light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Toccoa River is fair to good with recent stocking enhancing opportunities. Current flow is steady at 230 cfs with moderate conditions expected over the next 48 hours.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning offers the best fishing conditions due to moderate sky cover, low rain probability, and cooler temperatures supporting active trout near runs, seams, and structure with a balanced nymph approach.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS flow is 230 cfs, gage height is 1.21 ft, water temperature is unavailable, and turbidity data is not available.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current weather includes 30.06 inHg pressure with a stable trend, light west wind averaging 3.5 mph with gusts to 13.8 mph, sky cover averaging 29.6%, and a 63% chance of light rain totaling 0.1 inches.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor comfortable feeding lanes close to cover in runs, seams, and structure. Use a balanced nymph approach with clean drifts and natural patterns like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell attractors suited for recently stocked fish. Expect moderate activity with caution due to unavailable temperature and pressure trend data.
